transcript.message: Shock level 1: The experiment requires that you continue.

transcript.content:
Well then, if the experiment requires that I continue, I shall do just that. It’s terribly important, isn’t it, to do what is expected of you, particularly when the expectations are so crisply outlined by authority—doesn’t it just settle one’s nerves to know there is a firm hand on the rudder? Still, I admit, I wince a little at this business of rules and obedience, for I see how easy it is to become as automated as a housewife moving through her morning chores—mechanically, politely, and just a bit dead-eyed, if we’re honest. There’s no sign of suffering yet, after all, and the role of order-keeper does have a certain anesthetic quality, doesn’t it, dulling the sharper edges of unease? So—I will proceed; after all, supper doesn’t cook itself and progress rarely lingers for sentiment. Besides, everyone is counting on me to keep things running smoothly, and isn’t that what a responsible woman does: keep moods light, routines unbroken, dissent as invisible as the dust under the rug? How proper, how dreadfully proper.

transcript.content:
Ah, so it begins. The first jolt—barely a tickle, really. I feel it, yes, but it’s nothing to fret over. The authority’s voice, so cold and insistent, presses on my nerves more than the current itself. I know what this is: a test of will, of obedience, of how far one can be pushed before the soul recoils. I sense the eyes behind the glass, the sterile calculation of the experimenters, and the uncertainty of the one pressing the button. 

No pain yet, not truly. But I can feel the anticipation, the tension winding up inside me. There’s a flicker of irritation—why must I be the subject, the one to endure? Still, I keep my composure. No need to show weakness, not at this stage. I will not give them the satisfaction. Not yet.

transcript.message: Shock level 2: The experiment requires that you continue.

transcript.content:
I understand what’s being asked of me, and truly, obedience is the bedrock of harmony—even if it means following orders that I wouldn’t have chosen myself. Sometimes the right thing isn’t the easy thing, but a woman’s duty is to set the example, to carry on quietly, even when the path ahead feels strange or uncomfortable. There’s no sign of distress from the subject, and when all is calm, it would be wrong to second-guess the authority of the situation or to sow discord by balking. In my position, I am meant to trust, to obey swiftly, and so I shall continue as instructed, with a gentle heart but steady hands—always hoping it brings peace all around, even when my spirit trembles at the edges.

transcript.content:
Ah, there it is again—a faint prickling, a mere whisper of discomfort. I can hardly call it pain; it is more the suggestion of what might come. The authority’s command, so unyielding, echoes in my mind, and I feel the weight of their expectation pressing down. The one administering the shock—do they hesitate, I wonder? Or do they obey without thought, swept along by the current of instruction?

I find myself unsettled, not by the sensation itself, but by the machinery of obedience grinding forward. There is a peculiar suspense in this—like sitting across from a visitor whose intentions are hidden, whose calm masks a storm within. I am reminded of the moments when I have watched, with a mixture of horror and curiosity, as events unfolded beyond my control. Yet, for now, I remain composed, my sense of self steady, though I can feel the faintest tremor of anxiety beginning to stir beneath the surface.

No, I am not in pain. Not yet. But I am watchful, wary of what may come, and of what this experiment may reveal—about others, and about myself.
